paragraphs: You have an Egyptian toe form with very wide forefoot and heel. Throughout a wide profile.

Beyond the obvious: Your arch is particularly short, meaning your toes are relatively long. Especially when considering aggressive shoes look for sufficient height in the toe box to let your toes curl up. Your big toe drifts slightly inwards (could be a mild hallux valgus); we avoid very asymmetric lasts and prefer moderately asymmetric or wider-tipped designs that help the big toe stay in contact with the shoe tip.
interpretation.title: Your Foot Shape

paragraphs: Your Saltic Speed (EU 43, 0.5 down) is a typical fit for Saltic, where the usual downsize is one size.
Your Saltic Speed is built on a Roman toe form while you have Egyptian toes. The mismatch is the most likely cause; look for shoes with a Egyptian-compatible toe box.
interpretation.title: What Your Current Shoe Fit Tells Us

paragraphs: Based on your scan and current shoe fit, we target shoes with Egyptian toe form, wide forefoot width, and wide heel width.

Given your preference for trad and multipitch outdoors on granite and a comfort fit, we prioritize very stiff lace-ups with flat profile and no asymmetry, with ankle protection for trad/multipitch wear.
Your current shoes are soft enough to forgive some mismatch; stiffer recommendations may feel different at the same target geometry.
The softer, stiffer, and budget tiers below offer alternative options around this target.
interpretation.title: What to Look For

recommendations.description: Lace-up with Roman toe form, medium fit throughout. 4.5mm FS-Quattro rubber with a full, hard midsole, resulting in a firm shoe with flat profile and symmetric profile.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Tradeoffs: Roman toe form might not be a perfect fit for your Egyptian toe, and slightly narrower in the forefoot, slightly tighter in the heel, and slightly softer than we target.
